description:
You are an observability engineer doing a quick batch-fix on some draft Grafana dashboards that live in the workspace
/home/user/dashboards/new/.

Present situation
1. Inside /home/user/dashboards/new/ there are exactly three UTF-8 JSON files:
   • system_overview.json  
   • db_metrics.json  
   • queue_depth.json
2. Each file is a single-line JSON document that contains the key "uid" whose value starts with the prefix TEMP-
   (e.g. "uid":"TEMP-abc123").
3. The only thing that must be changed is that TEMP- must become PERM- while keeping the rest of the UID intact.

Your job
A) Use a combination of find and xargs (and whatever helper utilities you like: sed, awk …) to
   replace every occurrence of the string
        "uid":"TEMP-
   with
        "uid":"PERM-
   in every *.json file under /home/user/dashboards/new/.
   • Do NOT touch any other keys or change file names or paths.
   • All edits must be done in-place so that the files on disk end up containing the new PERM--prefixed UIDs.

B) Create a plain-text CSV log file
        /home/user/dashboard_fix.log
   with one line per dashboard file, no header row, using this exact field order:
        filename,OLD_UID,NEW_UID
   where
        filename  = the basename of the dashboard file, e.g. system_overview.json  
        OLD_UID   = the full UID string before modification, e.g. TEMP-abc123  
        NEW_UID   = the full UID string after  modification, e.g. PERM-abc123  
   • The lines must be sorted in lexicographical order by filename.
   • The file must end with a single trailing newline (i.e. the last line is terminated by ‘\n’).

When you are done the automated checker will
1. Open every JSON file in /home/user/dashboards/new/ and verify that the value of "uid" begins with PERM- instead of TEMP- and is otherwise unchanged.
2. Read /home/user/dashboard_fix.log and verify that it contains exactly three lines, sorted alphabetically by filename, matching the precise comma-separated format described above.
3. Cross-check that every OLD_UID in the log actually existed in its respective file before modification and that every NEW_UID matches what is now stored in the file.

You do NOT need to show your commands; only the resulting file edits and the log file must be correct.
truth:
Initial files present before the agent starts:

1. /home/user/dashboards/new/db_metrics.json  
   {"title":"Database Metrics","uid":"TEMP-db789","tags":["database"]}

2. /home/user/dashboards/new/queue_depth.json  
   {"title":"Queue Depth","uid":"TEMP-queue456","tags":["queues"]}

3. /home/user/dashboards/new/system_overview.json  
   {"title":"System Overview","uid":"TEMP-abc123","tags":["system"]}

Target state after successful completion:

The three dashboard files should contain (single-line JSON, identical ordering & spacing except the changed UID value):

/home/user/dashboards/new/db_metrics.json  
{"title":"Database Metrics","uid":"PERM-db789","tags":["database"]}

/home/user/dashboards/new/queue_depth.json  
{"title":"Queue Depth","uid":"PERM-queue456","tags":["queues"]}

/home/user/dashboards/new/system_overview.json  
{"title":"System Overview","uid":"PERM-abc123","tags":["system"]}

The log file must exist with exact content (including terminating newline):

/home/user/dashboard_fix.log  
db_metrics.json,TEMP-db789,PERM-db789  
queue_depth.json,TEMP-queue456,PERM-queue456  
system_overview.json,TEMP-abc123,PERM-abc123  
⏎ (implicit trailing newline)

No other files may be modified or created.
